subject: SCIENCE
title: Why Animals Matter
objective: Describe selected ecosystem roles and humane relationships.
introduction: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Why Animals Matter”.
A key word used in the lesson “Why Animals Matter”.
A key word used in the lesson “Why Animals Matter”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Every animal has the same role

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: What can some bees help do?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. turn stones into plants and make sunlight do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Why Animals Matter”?

options.text: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.
Every animal has the same role

items.explanation: The correct response is “Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.

options.text: Every animal has the same role

items.explanation: The correct response is “bees can pollinate” because it follows the lesson explanation: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: Every animal has the same role

items.explanation: The correct response is “Every animal has the same role” because it follows the lesson explanation: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.

items.prompt: What can some bees help do?
items.explanation: The correct response is “pollinate flowers” because it follows the lesson explanation: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Every animal has the same role

items.explanation: The correct response is “some animals disperse seeds” because it follows the lesson explanation: Animals can pollinate, disperse seeds, improve soil, provide food products, or be companions; treat them humanely.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Why Animals Matter; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Why Animals Matter.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
